text: Ultradian rhythms operate on timescales shorter than a day — most prominently the 90-to-110-minute oscillation that governs hormone secretion, gut contractions, nasal airflow, and neural firing patterns simultaneously. The synchrony is remarkable: growth hormone pulses roughly every 90 minutes, as does the shift between left- and right-hemisphere dominance in the brain. When the right hemisphere takes its turn at the front, cognitive style shifts toward holistic, associative thinking — the kind suited to synthesis and creative leaps rather than sequential analysis. Rest periods, from this view, are not empty intervals but phases of a different kind of intelligence.
question: According to the passage, what cognitive shift accompanies the 90-minute shift to right-hemisphere dominance?

options.text: Deeper capacity for sequential, logical analysis
A move toward holistic, associative thinking suited to creative leaps
Suppression of growth hormone to preserve focus
